rules.title: Substantive Pronouns - Overview
rules.text: Substantive pronouns in Slovak decline through all 7 cases just like nouns. They include personal pronouns (I, you, he, she, we, they), reflexive pronouns (myself, yourself), possessive pronouns used as nouns (mine, yours), demonstrative pronouns (this, that), interrogative pronouns (who, what), indefinite pronouns (someone, something), and negative pronouns (nobody, nothing).
examples: Personal: ja (I), ty (you), on (he), ona (she), my (we), vy (you pl.), oni (they)
Reflexive: sa/si (oneself)
Indefinite: niekto (someone), niečo (something), dakto (somebody)
Negative: nikto (nobody), nič (nothing)

rules.title: Personal Pronouns - Complete Declension
rules.text: Personal pronouns decline through all cases. Note: 1st and 2nd person have no gender distinction. 3rd person distinguishes masculine, feminine, and neuter.
examples: Nominative: Ja som tu – I am here
Genitive: Bez mňa – Without me
Dative: Daj mi to – Give it to me
Accusative: Vidíš ma? – Do you see me?
Locative: O mne – About me
Instrumental: So mnou – With me

rules.title: Personal Pronouns - Long vs Short Forms
rules.text: 1st and 2nd person pronouns have long (stressed) and short (unstressed/clitic) forms in genitive, dative, and accusative. Short forms are more common in everyday speech.
examples: Long (stressed): Bez mňa to neurobíš – Without ME you won't do it
Short (unstressed): Vidí ma – He sees me
Long: Daj to mne! – Give it to ME!
Short: Daj mi to – Give it to me

rules.title: Third Person Pronouns - Prepositional Forms
rules.text: 3rd person pronouns add 'n-' prefix after prepositions. This is mandatory and prevents confusion.
examples: Without prep: Vidím ho – I see him
With prep: Hovorím o ňom – I'm talking about him
Without prep: Dám mu knihu – I'll give him a book
With prep: Idem k nemu – I'm going to him
Without prep: Poznám ju – I know her
With prep: Hovorím o nej – I'm talking about her

rules.title: Reflexive Pronoun 'sa/si'
rules.text: The reflexive pronoun 'sa/si' refers back to the subject (myself, yourself, himself, herself, etc.). It has only accusative (sa) and dative (si) forms, no nominative. Used with reflexive verbs.
examples: Umývam sa – I wash myself
Učíš sa – You learn (yourself)
Kúpil si auto – You bought yourself a car
Zobral si to – He took it (for himself)
Pamätám si – I remember (to myself)
Myslím si – I think (to myself)

rules.title: Possessive Pronouns as Substantives
rules.text: Possessive pronouns can function as nouns (mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs) and decline like adjectives with gender agreement.
examples: To je môj – That's mine (masculine)
To je moja – That's mine (feminine)
Vidím tvojho – I see yours (masculine animate)
Hovorím o svojom – I'm talking about mine

rules.title: Demonstrative Pronouns - 'ten/tá/to' (this/that)
rules.text: Demonstrative pronouns 'ten' (this/that masculine), 'tá' (feminine), 'to' (neuter) decline through all cases.
examples: Nominative: Ten je dobrý – That one is good
Genitive: Cena toho – The price of that
Dative: Dám to tomu – I'll give it to that one
Accusative: Vidím toho – I see that one
Locative: O tom – About that
Instrumental: S tým – With that

rules.title: Interrogative Pronouns - 'kto/čo' (who/what)
rules.text: Interrogative pronouns 'kto' (who) and 'čo' (what) decline through all cases.
examples: Nominative: Kto je to? – Who is it?
Genitive: Koho vidíš? – Whom do you see?
Dative: Komu to dáš? – To whom will you give it?
Accusative: Koho poznáš? – Whom do you know?
Locative: O kom hovoríš? – About whom are you talking?
Instrumental: S kým ideš? – With whom are you going?

rules.title: Indefinite Pronouns - 'niekto/niečo' (someone/something)
rules.text: Indefinite pronouns 'niekto' (someone) and 'niečo' (something) decline like 'kto' and 'čo' with the prefix 'nie-'.
examples: Nominative: Niekto je tu – Someone is here
Genitive: Hľadám niekoho – I'm looking for someone
Dative: Dám to niekomu – I'll give it to someone
Accusative: Vidím niekoho – I see someone
Locative: Hovorím o niekom – I'm talking about someone
Instrumental: Idem s niekým – I'm going with someone

rules.title: Indefinite Pronouns - 'dakto/dačo' (somebody/something)
rules.text: Alternative indefinite pronouns 'dakto' (somebody) and 'dačo' (something) - more colloquial than 'niekto/niečo'.
examples: Dakto volal – Somebody called
Potrebujem dačo – I need something
Hovoril som s dakým – I spoke with somebody
Myslím na dakoho – I'm thinking about somebody

rules.title: Negative Pronouns - 'nikto/nič' (nobody/nothing)
rules.text: Negative pronouns 'nikto' (nobody) and 'nič' (nothing) decline like 'kto' and 'čo' with the prefix 'ni-'. Used with negative verbs.
examples: Nominative: Nikto tu nie je – Nobody is here
Genitive: Nevidím nikoho – I don't see anybody
Dative: Nedám to nikomu – I won't give it to anybody
Accusative: Nepoznám nikoho – I don't know anybody
Locative: Nehovorím o nikom – I'm not talking about anybody
Instrumental: Neidem s nikým – I'm not going with anybody

rules.title: Indefinite Pronouns - 'každý' (everyone/each)
rules.text: The pronoun 'každý' (everyone, each, every) declines like an adjective with gender agreement.
examples: Každý to vie – Everyone knows it
Hovorím s každým – I talk with everyone
Dám to každému – I'll give it to everyone
Vidím každého – I see everyone

rules.title: Indefinite Pronouns - 'všetko' (everything)
rules.text: The pronoun 'všetko' (everything, all) declines through all cases.
examples: Nominative: Všetko je tu – Everything is here
Genitive: Cena všetkého – The price of everything
Dative: Rozumiem všetkému – I understand everything
Accusative: Vidím všetko – I see everything
Locative: O všetkom – About everything
Instrumental: So všetkým – With everything

rules.title: Relative Pronoun - 'ktorý' (who/which/that)
rules.text: The relative pronoun 'ktorý' (who, which, that) introduces relative clauses and declines like an adjective.
examples: Muž, ktorý pracuje – The man who works
Žena, ktorú vidím – The woman whom I see
Dom, v ktorom bývam – The house in which I live
Priateľ, s ktorým idem – The friend with whom I go

rules.title: Pronoun 'sám' (self, alone)
rules.text: The emphatic pronoun 'sám' (self, alone, by oneself) declines like an adjective and emphasizes the subject.
examples: Urobil som to sám – I did it myself
Ona je sama doma – She is alone at home
Hovoril som so samým prezidentom – I spoke with the president himself
Sami to vieme – We know it ourselves

rules.title: Pronoun Usage Summary
rules.text: Quick reference for when to use each pronoun type.

rows: Ten je dobrý (That one is good)
Nikto tu nie je (Nobody is here)
Sám to urobím (I'll do it myself)
